concerns.text: T020 ('Implement scaffold-based cross-validation') is marked unchecked [ ]. This task is required to satisfy Spec FR-007 ('execute 5-fold cross-validation') and Plan.md's 'Statistical Rigor' (Constitution Principle VII). Without this task, the project cannot validate model generalizability as required by the spec.

T021 ('Implement scaling exponent analysis... test for power-law relationships') is present but not covered by any Spec FR/SC or Plan element. The spec limits scope to linear correlation and multivariate linear regression (FR-005, FR-007). This task introduces an unapproved research objective (universal scaling laws) not authorized in the plan, creating a scope creep risk.

T008 ('Setup directory structure... with checksum generation utility') is marked unchecked [ ]. The Plan.md and Constitution Principle III require raw data to be checksummed. T009 and T010 depend on this utility for data integrity. No other task implements the checksum logic, leaving a critical gap in the data hygiene pipeline.

T006a and T006 reference a 'RDKit vs. PyVib substitution' deviation. However, the Plan.md 'Spec Deviation & Governance' section explicitly defines DEV-001 as a reduction in 'Conformer ensemble size' (50 to 20), not a tool substitution. The task descriptions misalign with the actual approved governance record, creating a traceability gap where the code will log/verify the wrong deviation reason.

T022a, T022b, T023a, T023b, T024, T025 are all marked unchecked [ ]. T022a/T022b are required for FR-008 (visualization). T023a/T023b are required for FR-009 (associational flagging). T024/T025 are required for the 'Independent Test' criteria in US-3 (integration/contract tests). Missing these tasks prevents satisfying the spec's acceptance criteria for US-3.
